natural_occurrence.description: Although it occurs naturally in small amounts in citrus fruits and spinach, beta-apo-8'-carotenal used as a food additive is synthetically produced. It is a C30 carotenoid (shorter than the C40 structure of most carotenoids) with orange to orange-red color.

manufacturing.description: Produced by chemical synthesis rather than extraction from natural sources. The synthetic process creates a pure, standardized product with consistent coloring properties. While the compound exists in nature, commercial production is entirely synthetic.

other_uses.cosmetics: Used as a colorant in cosmetics and personal care products.
other_uses.pharmaceutical: Used as a colorant in pharmaceutical preparations.

eu.conditions: Permitted in specified food categories with maximum levels

usa.classification: Approved for use in foods generally
usa.notes: Maximum level: 15 mg per pound of solid/semisolid food or per pint of liquid food

japan.notes: Approved as a designated additive (synthetic colorant)

food_categories_eu.category_name: Fat emulsions mainly of type water-in-oil
Other confectionery including breath freshening microsweets

fun_facts: E160e is synthetic but chemically identical to the compound found naturally in oranges and citrus fruits.
It has approximately 50% of the pro-vitamin A activity of beta-carotene, meaning it can be partially converted to vitamin A in the body.
Beta-apo-8'-carotenal is a C30 carotenoid, making it smaller than most natural carotenoids which have a C40 structure.
This colorant is particularly stable in fatty foods and can help protect fats from oxidative rancidity, extending shelf life.
Despite being synthetic when used as an additive, it mimics natural carotenoid structures and functions.
health_benefits_disclaimer: While beta-apo-8'-carotenal has provitamin A activity and shows antioxidant properties in studies, it is used as a food colorant in very small quantities. Any potential health benefits should not be considered medical claims. Consult healthcare professionals for health advice.
